title: Lock stale takeover requires dead owner pid; 15min hard ceiling; long-hold regression test
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs: tryStaleTakeover currently fires on mtime age alone (STALE_MS=30s). Change (amended by advisor consult): takeover requires mtime>STALE_MS AND the recorded owner pid NOT alive — probe with process.kill(pid,0); EPERM counts as alive; missing/unparsable pid counts as dead. A pid that is provably ALIVE is never stolen below HARD_STALE_MS=3600000 (1h — far above any verify; pid-reuse guard applies only past this ceiling or when liveness is unknowable). Do NOT add a timer heartbeat (locked D2: spawnSync blocks the event loop so timers cannot fire during the exact long holds being protected). Update the header contract prose (L8-11): a live holder may legitimately hold across a long child spawn (worktree-admin merge verify); the 30s stale window is for crashed holders. scripts/test_store_lock.mjs: keep dead-holder takeover (pid 999999999); ADD (i) live-holder regression: lockfile with THIS process pid, mtime backdated 45s, must NOT be taken over; (ii) EPERM=alive unit case (monkeypatch or a root-owned pid probe fallback — if not feasible, document why and cover via unit of the liveness helper); (iii) past-ceiling: mtime backdated >1h with live pid MUST be taken over. ALSO add a worktree-merge-level regression (test_worktree_cli.mjs or test_worktree_store.mjs): while a merge holds worktree-admin across a slow verify (sleep-based fake verify >35s is enough, or inject a stub), a second worktree-admin acquisition attempt must NOT steal the lock (typed busy/refusal instead).

truths: A lockfile older than 30s whose pid is alive is not stolen
A lockfile older than 30s whose pid is dead is stolen (existing behavior preserved)
prohibitions: Do not change the lock file format in a way that breaks in-flight locks (extra fields OK)

trace.outcome: Lock stale takeover now requires the recorded owner pid to be dead (process.kill(pid,0); EPERM/unmapped-errno=alive, ESRCH/missing/unparsable=dead), gated below a new HARD_STALE_MS=1h absolute ceiling past which takeover proceeds regardless of liveness (pid-reuse guard of last resort). No timer heartbeat added (locked D2). Header contract prose updated. Added isPidAlive() unit coverage, a live-holder-not-stolen regression and a past-ceiling-taken-over regression to test_store_lock.mjs, plus a real bee-worktree-merge-level D2 regression to test_worktree_cli.mjs (slow 45s verify holding worktree-admin; a concurrent second acquisition attempt is refused, not stolen).

    "scripts/test_store_lock.mjs: scenario (f) past-ceiling regression -- lockfile owned by THIS process's real pid, mtime backdated past HARD_STALE_MS (1h) MUST be taken over regardless of liveness",

  "red_failure_evidence": "Constructed an intermediate pre-fix variant of lock.mjs (isPidAlive exported but tryStaleTakeover left as pure age-check, matching the exact pre-fix behavior) and ran scripts/test_store_lock.mjs against it: FAIL test_store_lock: (e) busy-attempt child against a 45s-backdated LIVE-pid lock exited 1, expected 0 (LOCK_BUSY refusal) -- a live holder's lock was likely stolen by age alone: stdout={\"ok\":false,\"error\":\"Error: critical section ran \\u2014 lock was NOT actually busy ...\"}; also flagged the live-holder lock file was removed/renamed, and the busy-attempt returned after only 49ms (too fast to have exercised the real retry budget) -- i.e. the lock was stolen almost instantly by age alone. This is the exact live-holder-steal bug the cell fixes.",
  "verification_run": "After restoring the real fix (isPidAlive gating tryStaleTakeover, HARD_STALE_MS=3_600_000 ceiling) to both skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs and .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs (byte-identical): node scripts/test_store_lock.mjs -> PASS (all scenarios a-f green, exit 0); node scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs -> SUMMARY 135/135 passed (exit 0), including the 8 new D2 regression rows. One transient scenario-(c) flake (3 mutual-exclusion violations) was observed once under concurrent multi-worker swarm load and did not reproduce across 3 immediate reruns plus the final recorded run -- consistent with the repo's known rerun-once timing-flake class (critical-patterns), and unrelated to the D2 liveness change (the dead-pid takeover path is unchanged in shape, only an added synchronous isPidAlive probe)."
}
trace.verify_output: test_store_lock: PASS isPidAlive() unit checks green; (a) 6x12 locked racers zero violations/exact count; (b) unguarded control demonstrated loss/overlap; (c) 6x12 racers survived a pre-seeded stale lock zero violations/exact count; (d) LOCK_BUSY refusal named real holder after real retry budget; (e) a 45s-backdated LIVE-pid lock was NOT stolen (LOCK_BUSY refusal); (f) 6x12 racers took over a live-but-past-HARD_STALE_MS-ceiling lock anyway, zero violations/exact count. test_worktree_cli.mjs: SUMMARY 135/135 passed, including 8 new D2 regression rows proving a slow-but-live merge holding worktree-admin across a 45s Atomics.wait verify is never stolen by a concurrent second acquisition attempt (typed busy refusal), and the original merge completes normally. Combined exit code 0. Note: one transient flake observed in scenario (c) under concurrent swarm load (3 mutual-exclusion violations), not reproduced across 3 immediate reruns plus this final recorded run -- consistent with repo's known rerun-once timing-flake class, unrelated to the D2 liveness logic (dead-pid path unchanged).

checks.evidence: lock.mjs:175-182 gates takeover: age<=STALE_MS false; between STALE_MS and HARD_STALE_MS reads holder.pid and returns false if isPidAlive; past ceiling steals regardless. isPidAlive: non-positive/unparsable dead, kill success alive, ESRCH dead, EPERM/other alive.
no setInterval/new timers in templates/lib/lock.mjs; only pre-existing promise sleep helper.

isPidAlive units incl EPERM=alive/ESRCH=dead/unmapped=alive via monkeypatch; scenario (e) live-holder 45s-backdate not stolen (LOCK_BUSY); scenario (f) past-ceiling live pid taken over; dead-holder takeover preserved.
real worktree merge holds worktree-admin across 45s Atomics.wait verify; second acquisition 33s past STALE_MS refused typed busy, zero mutation, original merge completes.

node scripts/test_store_lock.mjs green 3/3 rerun; one first-run scenario-(c) concurrency flake is the pre-existing takeover path under swarm load (documented rerun-once class), not the new liveness gate.
trace red_failure_evidence: pre-fix age-only variant let a busy child steal a live-holder lock in 49ms instead of LOCK_BUSY.
